description: Production-grade monitoring dashboard for Go microservices. 32 panels covering RED metrics, response time percentiles (P50/P95/P99), error rates, Apdex score, Go runtime health, memory leak detection, and service-level resource aggregation. Multi-service filtering with dynamic variables.

panels.title: 99th Percentile Response Success
panels.description: 99% of requests complete faster than this. Detects worst-case latency and outliers.

panels.title: 95th Percentile Response Success
panels.description: 95% of requests complete faster than this. Key metric for user experience.

panels.title: 50th Percentile Response Success
panels.description: Median response time. Represents typical user experience.

panels.title: Total RPS (All Requests)
panels.description: Total requests per second including all HTTP status codes (2xx, 4xx, 5xx). Use this to monitor overall traffic volume.

panels.title: Success RPS (2xx)
panels.description: Successful requests per second (HTTP 2xx responses). This represents productive traffic.

panels.title: Error RPS (4xx/5xx)
panels.description: Failed requests per second (HTTP 4xx/5xx responses). Monitor this to detect issues quickly.

panels.title: Success Rate %
panels.description: Percentage of successful requests (2xx / total).

panels.title: Error Rate %
panels.description: Percentage of failed requests (4xx/5xx / total).

targets.expr: (sum(rate(request_duration_seconds_bucket{app=~"$app", namespace=~"$namespace", job=~"microservices", le="0.5"}[$rate])) + 0.5 * (sum(rate(request_duration_seconds_bucket{app=~"$app", namespace=~"$namespace", job=~"microservices", le="2"}[$rate])) - sum(rate(request_duration_seconds_bucket{app=~"$app", namespace=~"$namespace", job=~"microservices", le="0.5"}[$rate])))) / (sum(rate(request_duration_seconds_count{app=~"$app", namespace=~"$namespace", job=~"microservices"}[$rate])) > 0 or vector(1))

panels.title: Apdex Score
panels.description: User satisfaction score (0-1). Satisfying: <0.5s, Tolerating: 0.5-2s, Frustrated: >2s. Formula: (satisfying + 0.5 * tolerating) / total requests. Handles zero traffic gracefully.

panels.title: Total Request
panels.description: Total requests in selected time range. Correlate with traffic spikes or incidents.

panels.title: Up Instances
panels.description: Number of healthy application instances. Monitor for scaling and failures.

panels.title: Restarts
panels.description: Pod restarts in time range. Frequent restarts indicate OOM or crashes.

panels.title: Status Code Distribution
panels.description: HTTP status code distribution by rate (req/sec). Shows real-time traffic breakdown by response code. Expected: ~95% codes 2xx.

panels.title: Total Requests by Endpoint
panels.description: Request distribution across endpoints. Identifies hot paths and traffic patterns.

panels.title: Request Rate by Endpoint
panels.description: Request rate per endpoint over time. Detects traffic spikes per API.

panels.title: RPS by Endpoint
panels.description: Request rate per endpoint path. Shows traffic distribution across different API endpoints over time.

panels.title: Request Rate by Method and Endpoint
panels.description: Breakdown of request rate by HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) and endpoint path. Helps identify traffic patterns and detect unusual method-specific spikes.

targets.expr: (sum(rate(request_duration_seconds_count{app=~"$app", namespace=~"$namespace", job=~"microservices", code=~"4..|5.."}[$rate])) by (method, path) / sum(rate(request_duration_seconds_count{app=~"$app", namespace=~"$namespace", job=~"microservices"}[$rate])) by (method, path)) * 100 > 0

panels.title: Error Rate by Method and Endpoint
panels.description: Percentage of 4xx/5xx errors by HTTP method and endpoint. Only displays combinations with error rate > 0%. Use this to quickly identify problematic endpoints.

panels.title: Client Errors (4xx)
panels.description: Client-side errors (400-499) in req/sec by service. High 4xx rates indicate API misuse, invalid requests, authentication issues, or bad client behavior. Common codes: 400 (Bad Request), 401 (Unauthorized), 403 (Forbidden), 404 (Not Found), 429 (Rate Limited).

panels.title: Server Errors (5xx)
panels.description: Server-side errors (500-599) in req/sec by service. High 5xx rates indicate service degradation, bugs, infrastructure issues, or dependency failures. Common codes: 500 (Internal Server Error), 502 (Bad Gateway), 503 (Service Unavailable), 504 (Gateway Timeout). Requires immediate investigation.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.95, sum(rate(request_duration_seconds_bucket{app=~"$app", namespace=~"$namespace", job=~"microservices"}[$rate])) by (le, path, code))

panels.title: Response time 95th percentile
panels.description: p95 response time per endpoint. Identifies slow APIs.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.50, sum(rate(request_duration_seconds_bucket{app=~"$app", namespace=~"$namespace", job=~"microservices"}[$rate])) by (le, path, code))

panels.title: Response time 50th percentile
panels.description: Median response time per endpoint. Shows typical performance baseline.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(request_duration_seconds_bucket{app=~"$app", namespace=~"$namespace", job=~"microservices"}[$rate])) by (le, path, code))

panels.title: Response time 99th percentile
panels.description: p99 response time per endpoint. Highlights tail latency issues.

panels.title: Heap Allocated Memory
panels.description: Memory currently allocated on heap. If steadily increasing without dropping after GC, indicates memory leak.

panels.title: Heap In-Use Memory
panels.description: Heap memory in use by app. Should return to baseline after GC. Steady growth indicates leak.

panels.title: Process Memory (RSS)
panels.description: Total physical memory used by process. Continuously increasing indicates memory leak at OS level.

panels.title: Goroutines & Threads
panels.description: Goroutine and OS thread count. Steadily increasing goroutines indicates goroutine leak (forgotten defer, unclosed channels). Stable count is normal.

panels.title: GC Duration
panels.description: GC pause duration. High values indicate memory pressure. Increases when heap is large.

panels.title: GC Frequency
panels.description: GC runs per second. High frequency indicates memory pressure or insufficient heap size.

panels.title: Total Memory per Service
panels.description: Total memory allocated by ALL pods in the service (aggregated). Example: user with 2 pods shows sum of both pods. Use for capacity planning and cost analysis.

panels.title: Total CPU per Service
panels.description: Total CPU usage by ALL pods in the service (aggregated). Use for scaling decisions: 'Should we add more pods?' Higher values indicate need for horizontal scaling.

panels.title: Total Network Traffic per Service
panels.description: Total HTTP traffic (TX/RX) by ALL pods in the service. Use for bandwidth planning and cost estimation. Note: Only HTTP body size, not TCP/IP overhead.

panels.title: Total Requests In Flight per Service
panels.description: Total concurrent requests being processed by ALL pods in the service. High values indicate saturation (4 Golden Signals). Use to detect bottlenecks and validate service capacity.

panels.title: Total Memory Allocations per Service
panels.description: Total memory frees by ALL pods in the service. Indicates GC activity. Use with GC Duration panel to detect memory pressure or allocation issues.

title: Microservices Observability Platform
